sections.label: Navigation Bar
sections.instruction: This is the strip at the very top of your page. Put your name or brand here — visitors see this first.

fields.name: logo
fields.label: Your Name or Brand
fields.instruction: Write your name or a short brand name. This shows in the top-left corner of your portfolio.

fields.name: ctaLabel
fields.label: Top Button Text
fields.instruction: The clickable button in the top-right corner. Keep it short and action-y — e.g. 'Hire Me', 'Work Together', 'Get in Touch'.

sections.label: Hero Section (Your Big Intro)
sections.instruction: This is the first big thing visitors see — your name dominates the screen in bold editorial style. Make it confident and clear.

fields.name: role
fields.label: What You Do (Job Title)
fields.instruction: Your role in a few words. This shows up big and bold under your name.

fields.name: descriptor
fields.label: One-Line Descriptor
fields.placeholder: Crafting digital products people actually love.
fields.instruction: A single punchy sentence that sums up your approach or philosophy. Think of it as your personal tagline.

fields.name: bio
fields.label: About You (Short Bio)
fields.placeholder: 6 years turning complex problems into elegant solutions. I care deeply about performance, accessibility, and the tiny details that make the difference between good and great.
fields.instruction: Write 2–3 sentences about yourself. What do you build? What makes you good at it? Be confident — this is your pitch.

fields.name: avatar
fields.label: Your Photo
fields.instruction: Upload one clear photo of yourself. It will appear in the hero section alongside your intro.

fields.name: tag1
fields.label: Expertise Tag 1
fields.instruction: A short keyword for your top skill or specialty. Shows as a highlighted tag near your name.

fields.name: tag2
fields.label: Expertise Tag 2
fields.instruction: A second skill tag. Keep it short — one word or a short phrase.

fields.name: tag3
fields.label: Expertise Tag 3
fields.instruction: A third skill tag. Together the three tags give visitors a quick snapshot of what you do.

fields.name: stat1Label
fields.label: Stat 1 Label
fields.instruction: The label for your first stat. e.g. 'Years Building', 'Years Experience'.

fields.name: stat1Value
fields.label: Stat 1 Value
fields.instruction: The number or value for Stat 1. e.g. '6' or '6+'.

fields.name: stat2Label
fields.label: Stat 2 Label
fields.instruction: The label for your second stat. e.g. 'Projects Shipped', 'Apps Built'.

fields.name: stat2Value
fields.label: Stat 2 Value
fields.instruction: The number or value for Stat 2. e.g. '50+' or '30'.

fields.name: stat3Label
fields.label: Stat 3 Label
fields.instruction: The label for your third stat. e.g. 'Happy Clients', 'Satisfied Clients'.

fields.name: stat3Value
fields.label: Stat 3 Value
fields.instruction: The number or value for Stat 3. e.g. '20+' or '15'.

fields.name: resumeLink
fields.label: Link to Your CV / Resume
fields.instruction: Paste the full link to your CV. Upload it to Google Drive and share the link. Must start with https://

sections.label: Skills
sections.instruction: Group your skills by category. Each group becomes one card. You can add up to 8 groups. Example groups: Frontend, Backend, Tools, Design.

fields.name: title
fields.label: Skill Category Name
fields.instruction: What type of skills are in this group? e.g. Frontend, Backend, Tools, Design.

fields.name: skills
fields.label: Skills in This Category
fields.instruction: List your skills separated by commas. These appear as tags on the card.

sections.label: Work Experience
sections.instruction: List the jobs you've had. Each job becomes a large numbered timeline entry. Put newest jobs first. If you have no experience yet, just turn the section off below.

fields.name: showExperience
fields.label: Show Work Experience Section?
fields.instruction: Turn this OFF if you have no work experience yet. The section will be completely hidden from your portfolio.

fields.name: role
fields.label: Your Job Title
fields.instruction: What was your role? e.g. Junior Developer, Senior Engineer, Freelance Developer, Intern.

fields.name: company
fields.label: Company or Client Name
fields.instruction: Name of the company or client. If you freelanced, write the client's name or just 'Self-employed'.

fields.name: period
fields.label: When Did You Work There?
fields.instruction: Write the time period naturally. e.g. 'Jan 2021 – Present' or '2020 – 2022'.

fields.name: location
fields.label: Location
fields.instruction: City name or 'Remote' if you worked online.

fields.name: description
fields.label: What Did You Do There?
fields.placeholder: Rebuilt the core rendering pipeline, cutting frame drop rate by 80% on low-end devices.
fields.instruction: Describe your work in 2–3 sentences. What did you build? What did you achieve? Numbers help — e.g. 'reduced latency by 80%'.

sections.label: Projects
sections.instruction: Your main work showcase. Each project gets a full-width editorial card with an image, description, and links. Add up to 12 projects. Your best work goes first.

fields.name: title
fields.label: Project Name
fields.instruction: The name of your project.

fields.name: date
fields.label: Year Built
fields.instruction: Just the year, or a range like '2023 – 2024'.

fields.name: description
fields.label: What Is This Project?
fields.placeholder: Event-driven analytics engine processing 1M+ events/day with sub-100ms query times. Built entirely in TypeScript.
fields.instruction: Explain what the project does and what makes it interesting. 2–3 sentences max. What problem does it solve?

fields.name: image
fields.label: Project Screenshot
fields.instruction: Upload a screenshot or preview image of your project. Wide (landscape) images look best. This is the first thing visitors notice on the card.

fields.name: tags
fields.label: Technologies Used
fields.instruction: What did you build it with? Separate with commas.

fields.name: github
fields.label: GitHub Link
fields.instruction: Link to the project's GitHub repository. Only add this if the repo is public.

fields.name: webapp
fields.label: Live Project Link
fields.instruction: Link to the live hosted version of your project. If it's not hosted yet, leave this empty.

fields.name: showMembers
fields.label: Did You Build This With a Team?
fields.instruction: Turn ON if this was a group project. You can then add your teammates' names and GitHub links.

sections.label: Education
sections.instruction: Your academic background. Each entry becomes a clean list card. Online courses count too — add them if you have no formal degree. Up to 6 entries.

fields.name: degree
fields.label: Degree or Course Name
fields.instruction: The full name of your degree or course. e.g. 'B.Sc. Computer Science' or 'Full Stack Web Dev Bootcamp (Udemy)'.

fields.name: institution
fields.label: University, College, or Platform
fields.instruction: Where did you study? For online courses, write the platform name e.g. Udemy, Coursera.

fields.name: period
fields.label: When Did You Study?
fields.instruction: Write the years. e.g. '2020 – 2024' or just '2023' for a short course.

fields.name: grade
fields.label: Grade or Distinction (Optional)
fields.instruction: Your grade, GPA, or any honours. Skip this field if you'd rather not show it.

fields.name: description
fields.label: Extra Notes (Optional)
fields.placeholder: Thesis: Optimising graph traversal algorithms for social network analysis.
fields.instruction: Anything extra worth mentioning — your thesis topic, a club, a specialization, or a major achievement.

sections.label: Certifications
sections.instruction: Certificates you've earned — Udemy, Coursera, AWS, Google, anything counts. Each one becomes a bold list item. Up to 12 certifications.

fields.name: showCertifications
fields.label: Show Certifications Section?
fields.instruction: Turn this OFF if you have no certifications. The section will be hidden from your portfolio.

fields.name: name
fields.label: Certificate Name
fields.instruction: The official name of the certificate exactly as it appears on the certificate.

fields.name: issuer
fields.label: Who Gave You This Certificate?
fields.instruction: The platform or organization that issued it. e.g. AWS, Google, Coursera, Meta, Udemy.

fields.name: date
fields.label: When Did You Get It?
fields.instruction: Month and year is enough. e.g. 'August 2023'.

fields.name: link
fields.label: Verification Link (Optional)
fields.instruction: The shareable link to verify your certificate. Udemy, Coursera, and Credly give you one after completion. Paste it here so people can check it.

sections.label: Contact
sections.instruction: How people get in touch with you. At minimum, add your email. Only add social links for platforms you actually use.

fields.name: email
fields.label: Your Email Address
fields.instruction: Use an email you actually check. This is how clients will contact you.

fields.name: github
fields.label: GitHub Profile Link
fields.instruction: Your full GitHub profile URL. e.g. https://github.com/kaioduya

fields.name: showTwitter
fields.label: Show Twitter on Portfolio?
fields.instruction: Turn OFF if you don't want Twitter shown.

fields.name: showFacebook
fields.label: Show Facebook on Portfolio?
fields.instruction: Turn ON if you want Facebook shown.

fields.name: showInstagram
fields.label: Show Instagram on Portfolio?
fields.instruction: Turn ON if you want Instagram shown.

fields.name: location
fields.label: Your Location
fields.instruction: Your city or 'Remote' if you work online. Clients like knowing where you're based.

fields.name: ctaHeadline
fields.label: Contact Section Tagline
fields.instruction: A short friendly line that appears above your contact info. Think of it as an invitation to reach out.

sections.label: Footer
sections.instruction: The very last line at the bottom of your page. Usually your name and the year.

fields.name: text
fields.label: Footer Text
fields.instruction: One short line. Keep it simple — your name and year is enough, or add a personal touch.
